backlog.title: #@file Whole-File Injection Extension (MVP)
backlog.description: Deliver a single-file TypeScript extension for Pi (@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent v0.80.7) that unconditionally injects entire file contents into the model's prompt when the user writes #@<path>. The extension hooks the 'input' event (fires for all prompt contexts: interactive TUI, initial CLI/-p message, RPC), reads the referenced file in full, and appends its contents in <file> tag blocks below the original prompt text. No size limits, no configuration, no dependencies beyond Pi's exported APIs. Target: ~150-220 lines in sharp-at-file.ts.

milestones.title: Core Extension Implementation
milestones.description: Implement the complete sharp-at-file.ts extension: file scaffold with verified imports and constants, token parsing and path resolution helpers, file-type detection and format helpers, the core injectFiles assembly function, and the input event handler with all safety guards (loop prevention, steer skip, pre-check). All APIs verified against the installed package (see architecture/api_verification.md).

tasks.title: Extension Scaffold, Constants & Parsing Helpers
tasks.description: Create the sharp-at-file.ts file with verified imports, module-level constants (detection regex, MIME table, trailing punctuation set), and the pure helper functions for token cleanup, tilde expansion + path resolution, and extension extraction. The factory is registered as a stub that will be filled by T3.S2.

subtasks.title: Extension file scaffold: imports, constants, factory stub
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Pi extensions load via 'jiti' (transpile-on-load, NO tsconfig needed). The default export must be a function (pi: ExtensionAPI) => void. Imports from @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent and @earendil-works/pi-ai are virtualized/aliased by Pi's loader — no local package.json or node_modules needed. See architecture/external_deps.md for the verified import pattern (all 6 API claims VERIFIED). See architecture/api_verification.md for exact type definitions with file:line evidence.
2. INPUT: No prior subtask — this is the file foundation. PRD §8 (File Structure) and Appendix A (Minimal skeleton) define the target structure.
3. LOGIC: Create ./sharp-at-file.ts with:
   - Imports per §7: 'import type { ExtensionAPI } from "@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent"', 'import type { ImageContent } from "@earendil-works/pi-ai"', 'import { resizeImage, formatDimensionNote } from "@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent"', 'import { promises as fs } from "node:fs"', 'import * as path from "node:path"', 'import * as os from "node:os"'.
   - Constants per §8.2 and §4.2: FILE_INJECT_RE = /(^|(?<=\W))#@(\S+)/g (the zero-width anchor (^|(?<=\W)) consumes NOTHING so m[0] is exactly #@<path>). MIME_BY_EXT = { png: "image/png", jpg: "image/jpeg", jpeg: "image/jpeg", gif: "image/gif", webp: "image/webp", bmp: "image/bmp" } per §5.2. TRAILING_PUNCT = ".,;:!?\")\]}>'" per §4.3.
   - Factory skeleton per Appendix A: 'export default function (pi: ExtensionAPI) { pi.on("input", async (event, ctx) => { return { action: "continue" }; }); }' — stub body (pass-through) that T3.S2 will replace with the real handler logic.
4. OUTPUT: A loadable extension file at ./sharp-at-file.ts. Pi must be able to import it without errors ('pi -e ./sharp-at-file.ts'). The factory registers an input handler that currently passes through (continue). Constants (FILE_INJECT_RE, MIME_BY_EXT, TRAILING_PUNCT) are module-level and available to all subsequent subtasks in the same file.
5. DOCS: none — internal scaffold, no user-facing/config/API surface change yet.

subtasks.title: Token parsing & path resolution helpers: cleanToken, expandTildeAndResolve, extOf
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The detection regex uses a zero-width lookbehind (^|(?<=\W)) — this consumes NOTHING, so m[0] is exactly '#@<path>' with no leading-char bookkeeping. When iterating with matchAll() there is no lastIndex stale issue (unlike test()/exec() with global flag — see architecture/system_context.md §Canonical Reference Pattern). Tilde expansion uses os.homedir() directly because the built-in resolveReadPath() is NOT exported (internal only — see architecture/system_context.md §3.3 and api_verification.md). path.resolve(cwd, expanded) handles absolute, relative, and ../ paths.
2. INPUT: The module-level constants from T1.S1 (FILE_INJECT_RE, TRAILING_PUNCT) in the same file ./sharp-at-file.ts.
3. LOGIC: Implement three pure helper functions in ./sharp-at-file.ts:
   - cleanToken(raw: string): string (§4.3) — repeatedly remove trailing chars found in TRAILING_PUNCT from the end of the string until none remain. Return empty string if all chars trimmed. This preserves file.txt, ~/a/b, ./x, ../y while trimming trailing periods/commas/parens/etc.
   - expandTildeAndResolve(p: string, cwd: string): string (§4.4) — if p === '~' return os.homedir(); if p.startsWith('~/') return path.join(os.homedir(), p.slice(2)); else use p as-is. Then return path.resolve(cwd, expanded). No cwd restriction — absolute, ~, and ../ paths all allowed (same trust model as built-in read tool).
   - extOf(abs: string): string — extract path.basename(abs), find lastIndexOf('.'), return lowercased slice after the dot; return '' if no dot or dot at position 0 (hidden files like '.bashrc' have ext '').
4. OUTPUT: Three exported pure functions available to T2.S1 and T3.S1. cleanToken returns a trimmed path token or ''. expandTildeAndResolve returns an absolute filesystem path string. extOf returns a lowercased extension string or ''.
5. DOCS: none — internal helpers, no user-facing surface.

tasks.title: File-Type Detection & Format Helpers
tasks.description: Implement the pure helper functions for binary detection (NUL-byte heuristic) and the three <file> block format functions (text, image, binary). These match the exact output format of Pi's built-in processFileArguments for text/image cases and add a dedicated binary note for non-image binaries.

subtasks.title: Binary detection + text/image/binary format block helpers
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The built-in processFileArguments (dist/cli/file-processor.js) has NO binary guard — non-image binaries fall through to UTF-8 text read (garbage). This extension's NUL-byte check (§5.1) is a deliberate improvement. VERIFIED format templates from architecture/extension_patterns.md §2: TEXT → '<file name="' + absolutePath + '">\n' + content + '\n</file>\n'; IMAGE → push ImageContent + '<file name="' + absolutePath + '">' + hints + '</file>\n' (empty hints = '<file name="ABS"></file>'). formatDimensionNote (verified signature: (result: ResizedImage) => string | undefined — returns undefined when image was NOT resized, i.e. wasResized===false). The binary note format is custom (no built-in equivalent): '<file name="ABS"><binary file — contents not injected; use the read tool if needed></file>'.
2. INPUT: The formatDimensionNote import from T1.S1 (from @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent). The ResizedImage type (imported or inferred from resizeImage's return type) for the formatImageBlock parameter.
3. LOGIC: Implement four pure helper functions in ./sharp-at-file.ts:
   - isBinary(buf: Buffer): boolean (§5.1) — scan the first min(buf.length, 8000) bytes for a 0x00 NUL byte; return true if found. This is for ROUTING non-image binaries to the binary note, NOT for size gating. Image files skip this check entirely (classified by MIME first in injectFiles).
   - formatTextFileBlock(abs: string, content: string): string (§6.1) — return '<file name="' + abs + '">\n' + content + '\n</file>' — EXACT match to processFileArguments text format (minus trailing \n which is handled by assembly join).
   - formatImageBlock(abs: string, resized: ResizedImage | null): string (§5.2/§6.1) — call formatDimensionNote(resized) if resized is not null; return '<file name="' + abs + '">' + (hint ?? '') + '</file>'.
   - formatBinaryBlock(abs: string): string (§5.3/§6.1) — return '<file name="' + abs + '"><binary file — contents not injected; use the read tool if needed></file>'.
4. OUTPUT: Four exported pure functions. Each format function returns a string in <file> tag format. isBinary returns boolean. These are consumed by injectFiles in T3.S1.
5. DOCS: none — internal helpers. Format parity with processFileArguments is verified in T4.S1.

tasks.title: Core injectFiles Assembly & Input Event Handler
tasks.description: Implement the central injectFiles function that orchestrates token matching, file classification, I/O, and output assembly — and wire it into the input event handler with all safety guards (loop prevention, steer skip, cheap pre-check, error containment).

subtasks.title: injectFiles: core assembly function (token iteration, file dispatch, block/image assembly)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: CRITICAL — the input runner chains handlers in load order (architecture/system_context.md §Input Event Dispatch Internals). A 'transform' return with 'images' present REPLACES the array. Therefore images MUST be seeded as [...imagesIn] and returned in full — returning only new images would DESTROY any user-attached images. resizeImage takes Uint8Array (param named 'inputBytes' in dist — wrap Buffer: new Uint8Array(buf)). It is ASYNC and spawns a Worker thread (Photon WASM). Returns null if it can't process → fall back to raw buf.toString('base64'). Image files skip the NUL-byte check entirely (classified by MIME type first, not by binary detection). IMPORTANT divergence from processFileArguments: empty files (0 bytes) MUST be injected as '<file name="…">\n\n</file>' — do NOT skip them (processFileArguments skips empty files with 'if (stats.size === 0) continue' — this extension intentionally injects them per §10 edge cases).
2. INPUT: From T1.S2: cleanToken(raw) → string, expandTildeAndResolve(p, cwd) → string, extOf(abs) → string. From T2.S1: isBinary(buf) → boolean, formatTextFileBlock(abs, content) → string, formatImageBlock(abs, resized) → string, formatBinaryBlock(abs) → string. From T1.S1: FILE_INJECT_RE constant, MIME_BY_EXT constant, resizeImage import. The ctx.cwd string (from ExtensionContext, verified: ctx.cwd is a string per architecture/api_verification.md §5).
3. LOGIC: Implement 'async function injectFiles(text: string, imagesIn: ImageContent[], ctx: { cwd: string }): Promise<{ text: string; images: ImageContent[]; injected: number }>' per §9 pseudocode:
   (a) Initialize: blocks: string[] = [], images = [...imagesIn] (MERGE, do not replace), count = 0.
   (b) Iterate 'for (const m of text.matchAll(FILE_INJECT_RE))': get raw = m[2] (capture group 2 = path token after #@), call cleanToken(raw), skip (continue) if empty.
   (c) Resolve: abs = expandTildeAndResolve(token, ctx.cwd).
   (d) Stat: try { st = await fs.stat(abs) } catch { continue } — missing file → leave token verbatim. Check st.isFile() → if false (directory, socket, etc.) → continue (leave token).
   (e) Classify: ext = extOf(abs), mime = MIME_BY_EXT[ext].
   (f) IMAGE path (mime is truthy, §5.2): try { buf = await fs.readFile(abs); resized = await resizeImage(new Uint8Array(buf), mime); images.push({ type: "image", data: resized?.data ?? buf.toString("base64"), mimeType: resized?.mimeType ?? mime }); blocks.push(formatImageBlock(abs, resized)); count++ } catch { continue }
   (g) TEXT/BINARY path (§5.1/§5.3): try { buf = await fs.readFile(abs); if (isBinary(buf)) { blocks.push(formatBinaryBlock(abs)) } else { blocks.push(formatTextFileBlock(abs, buf.toString("utf8"))) }; count++ } catch { continue }
   (h) IMPORTANT: each file's processing (stat+read+resize) is wrapped in its OWN try/catch. On error: leave that token verbatim (do NOT append a block), continue processing remaining tokens. Never throw.
   (i) If count === 0 → return { text, images: imagesIn, injected: 0 } (return ORIGINAL images array, not the copy — nothing changed).
   (j) Else: const finalText = text + '\n\n---\n\n' + blocks.join('\n\n'); return { text: finalText, images, injected: count }.
   (k) CRITICAL: the ORIGINAL prompt text is NOT modified — #@path markers remain in place. Blocks are APPENDED after '\n\n---\n\n'. The join separator between blocks is '\n\n'.
4. OUTPUT: The injectFiles function. Returns { text, images, injected } where 'injected' is a numeric count (0 means nothing was injected → caller returns 'continue'). Non-zero count means blocks were appended and/or images were attached → caller returns 'transform'.
5. DOCS: none — internal core function. Extension behavior is documented in README (T5.S1).

subtasks.title: Input event handler: replace factory stub with real handler (guards, injectFiles call, notify, transform/continue)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The input event fires inside AgentSession.prompt() for ALL input contexts (interactive TUI, initial CLI/-p message, RPC) — this is the KEY ADVANTAGE over Pi's built-in @file CLI expansion which runs pre-prompt() during argv parsing (architecture/system_context.md, architecture/extension_patterns.md §1 §3). THREE mandatory guards: (a) event.source === 'extension' → MUST return continue to prevent infinite loops if any extension re-feeds #@ text (§12.1 — MANDATORY, architecture/extension_patterns.md §3 input-transform.ts demonstrates this pattern); (b) event.streamingBehavior === 'steer' → return continue for latency during mid-stream steering (mirrors input-transform-streaming.ts, architecture/extension_patterns.md §3); (c) !event.text?.includes('#@') → cheap pre-check before any regex/IO work (§12.4). The runner swallows per-handler errors (logged via emitError, chain continues — architecture/system_context.md §Input Event Dispatch Internals) but the extension should STILL never throw. ctx.ui.notify(message, type?) — parameter is named 'type' (not 'level'), constrained to 'info'|'warning'|'error' (architecture/api_verification.md §5). Guard notify with ctx.hasUI for print/json modes (architecture/api_verification.md §5: hasUI is true in TUI and RPC modes only).
2. INPUT: The injectFiles function from T3.S1. The factory stub from T1.S1 (currently: 'pi.on("input", async (event, ctx) => { return { action: "continue" }; })'). The ExtensionAPI type and InputEvent/InputEventResult types.
3. LOGIC: Replace the factory stub body in ./sharp-at-file.ts with the real handler per §9 and Appendix A:
   - 'if (event.source === "extension") return { action: "continue" };' (loop prevention — MANDATORY)
   - 'if (event.streamingBehavior === "steer") return { action: "continue" };' (latency during steering)
   - 'if (!event.text?.includes("#@")) return { action: "continue" };' (cheap pre-check)
   - 'const { text, images, injected } = await injectFiles(event.text, event.images ?? [], ctx);'
   - 'if (!injected) return { action: "continue" };' (nothing injected → preserve prompt byte-for-byte)
   - 'if (ctx.hasUI) ctx.ui.notify(`#@ injected ${injected} file(s)`, "info");' (user feedback, guarded for headless modes)
   - 'return { action: "transform" as const, text, images };' (rewrite prompt with injected content + merged images)
4. OUTPUT: A complete, functional extension. The factory registers an input handler that short-circuits on extension-source/steer/no-#@, delegates to injectFiles, and returns 'transform' with expanded text + merged images, or 'continue' if nothing was injected. The extension is loadable and testable.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Add a JSDoc comment block above 'export default function' describing: the extension purpose, the #@<path> trigger syntax, that it works in all input contexts (interactive/CLI/RPC), and that there are no size limits or configuration. This is the public-facing entry point of the extension.

milestones.title: Acceptance Validation & Documentation
milestones.description: Validate the completed extension against the PRD §11 acceptance criteria (14-case manual test matrix including format parity with processFileArguments and @ non-interference). Then sync changeset-level documentation (README.md) covering usage, behavior, and known limitations.

tasks.title: Acceptance Criteria Validation
tasks.description: Execute the complete 14-case manual test matrix from PRD §11 to validate that the extension meets all acceptance criteria: file injection works in all contexts, format parity with built-in @file, correct handling of images/binaries/missing/directories, no interference with bare @ syntax, and no uncaught errors.

subtasks.title: Manual test matrix: all 14 acceptance cases including format parity and @ non-interference
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Load the extension with 'pi -e ./sharp-at-file.ts' for quick testing, or copy to ~/.pi/agent/extensions/sharp-at-file.ts and use /reload (architecture/system_context.md §Extension Loading Mechanism). The input event fires in prompt() for ALL contexts — so #@ works in interactive TUI, initial 'pi -p "text #@file"' CLI, and RPC (architecture/system_context.md §Canonical Reference Pattern, architecture/extension_patterns.md §1). Format parity (test #13): compare against 'pi @a.ts "x"' CLI built-in output — both should emit '<file name="/abs/a.ts">\n<content>\n</file>'. processFileArguments emits blocks with trailing \n; our extension uses blocks.join('\n\n') so the internal separator differs slightly but the per-block format must match.
2. INPUT: The complete extension from T3.S2 (all prior subtasks implemented). Test fixtures to create: (a) small text file a.ts (~50 words), (b) a large file huge.log (~50MB or smaller representative), (c) a PNG image pic.png, (d) a binary file data.bin (with NUL bytes — e.g. write raw bytes), (e) a directory src/, (f) a tilde-path file ~/notes.md, (g) a second text file b.ts for multi-file test.
3. LOGIC: Execute the 14-case test matrix from §11 (h3.19) and verify each expected behavior:
   - Test 1: 'Review #@a.ts' → prompt contains a.ts contents in <file> block; NO read tool call; original prompt text unchanged; block appended after ---.
   - Test 2: 'Summarize #@huge.log' → ENTIRE file injected, no truncation.
   - Test 3: 'Describe #@pic.png' → ImageContent attached; <file name=...> reference appended; marker stays in text.
   - Test 4: 'Inspect #@data.bin' → binary note block appended; no decoded garbage.
   - Test 5: 'Fix #@nope.ts' → token left verbatim; prompt otherwise unchanged.
   - Test 6: 'List #@src/' → token left verbatim (directory).
   - Test 7: 'the foo#@bar thing' → NO expansion (mid-word, #@ preceded by word char 'o').
   - Test 8: '# Heading and #1234' → NO expansion (no #@).
   - Test 9: 'Diff #@a.ts vs #@b.ts' → both injected; notify says '2 file(s)'.
   - Test 10: 'Read #@~/notes.md' → tilde-expanded; injected.
   - Test 11: 'See #@a.ts.' → period trimmed; a.ts injected.
   - Test 12: 'pi -p "Review #@a.ts"' (extension loaded) → a.ts injected in the -p run too.
   - Test 13 (FORMAT PARITY): compare '#@a.ts' output block format vs 'pi @a.ts "x"' CLI output — both emit '<file name="/abs/a.ts">\n<content>\n</file>' with identical content.
   - Test 14 (@ NON-INTERFERENCE): 'Review @a.ts' interactive → @a.ts left as literal text (Pi's existing behavior preserved); NO injection by this extension.
   For EACH case: document pass/fail, any deviations from expected, and any uncaught errors. The extension is 'done' when all 14 pass with: no uncaught errors, model receives whole-file contents with zero read tool calls for #@-injected files, prompts without #@ (including bare @file) are byte-for-byte unchanged, and #@ works in both interactive and initial -p messages.
4. OUTPUT: A pass/fail validation report for all 14 test cases. Any bugs found feed back into the implementing subtasks (T1-T3) for fixes. The extension meets Definition of Done when all 14 pass.
5. DOCS: none — validation only. Findings inform the README known-limitations section in T5.S1.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Final documentation task (Mode B per SOW §5): create README.md covering the extension's purpose, installation, usage examples, behavior summary, known limitations, and relationship to Pi's existing @ syntax. This depends on all implementing subtasks and the validation results.

subtasks.title: Create README.md: extension purpose, installation, usage, behavior, known limitations
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: This is a Mode B documentation task (SOW §5) — cross-cutting docs that only make sense once the whole change is in place. The extension has NO config surface (no env vars, no config files, no CLI flags beyond -e) so there are no CONFIGURATION.md updates needed. Known limitations to document (from §4.4, §4.3, §13.2): (1) paths with spaces cannot be expressed — a space ends the token; users use the read tool instead. (2) #@ inside a fenced code block is still matched/injected — use '# @' with a space to avoid. (3) No size limit — large files can blow the context window; this is the documented, accepted tradeoff. (4) #@some/dir/ is left as a literal token (no directory reads). The extension also does NOT replace @ (§13.5): #@ = whole file always; @ = existing Pi behavior (autocomplete interactive, inject at CLI) unchanged.
2. INPUT: The validated extension from T4.S1 (all 14 acceptance criteria passing). PRD §1 (Overview/Solution/Value/Tagline), §2 (Goals/Non-Goals), §4 (Syntax spec), §10 (Edge cases), §13 (Design rationale/tradeoffs). The validation report from T4.S1 for any additional findings.
3. LOGIC: Create ./README.md with the following sections:
   - Title + tagline from §1.3: '#@file — inject the whole file, every time, everywhere.'
   - What it does (§1.1/§1.2): a dedicated #@<path> syntax that unconditionally injects the ENTIRE contents of a file into the model's context at prompt-submission time. Works in every context (interactive TUI, initial CLI/-p message, RPC) because it hooks the input event.
   - Installation: copy sharp-at-file.ts to ~/.pi/agent/extensions/ (global) or .pi/extensions/ (project-local), or use 'pi -e ./sharp-at-file.ts' for quick testing. Reload with /reload.
   - Usage examples: 'Review #@a.ts', 'Describe #@pic.png', 'Summarize #@~/notes.md', 'Diff #@a.ts vs #@b.ts'.
   - Behavior summary table (§5): text files → full content in <file> block; images → attached as image with reference tag; binary → note block; missing/dir → token left verbatim.
   - Key design choices (§13): why #@ is separate from @ (§13.3), why no size limit (§13.1/§13.2), why no config (§13.4).
   - Known limitations section (§4.4, §4.3, §13.2): paths with spaces, code-block matching, no size gate, no directories, no globbing.
   - Relationship to @ (§13.5): #@ = whole file always; @ = existing Pi behavior; they coexist.
4. OUTPUT: ./README.md — comprehensive user-facing documentation for the extension.
5. DOCS: This IS the documentation task (Mode B). Creates README.md as the changeset-level doc sweep.
